title: OIDC replay protection evolved from process-local evaluation to an atomic durable-store contract
summary: The first Cloudflare integration evaluated replay state supplied to one process and explicitly retained a durability limitation. Frontier Research then introduced a uniqueness-constrained SQL contract and adversarial concurrency tests, followed by a bounded temporal policy. The durable project memory is therefore not 'replay protection was added' as one undifferentiated event: process-local classification was an intermediate implementation, atomic cross-run acceptance is the stronger design, and deployment/runtime proof remains separate.

status_relations.unresolved: No retained production transaction proves that two concurrent workflow invocations were serialized by a deployed durable backend.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the four cited commits. Reject this record if the first integration already performs an atomic durable transaction, if the later store lacks a uniqueness constraint or concurrency test, or if retained production evidence demonstrates a different implementation sequence.
